title: Change log level for health_check
body:
Filter health_check and exec events for logging in console

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->
Podman server logs are mostly full of healthcheck output, making them hard to navigate. Hence, made healthcheck service to run with `LogLevelMax=notice,` this would remove the normal output, inclusive the started/stopped messages from systemd itself.

Fixes #17856 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Podman events' logs in systemd will output limited events in following ways:

- Podman healthcheck run would not emit `exec` and `exec_died` events

```

comments.body: Code change LGTM, test needs work

Halfhearted suggestion:
```go
		// Test hc logs to not include exec-died events
		hcLogs := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"events", "--stream=false", "--filter", "container=hc", "--since", "1m"})
		hcLogs.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(hcLogs).Should(ExitCleanly())
		Expect(hcLogs.OutputToString()).ToNot(ContainSubstring("exec_died"))
```
"halfhearted" because this test actually doesn't work: I see `exec_died` events in my log. Offering it as a start.

(A better test would use the CID or a pseudorandom container name, `"hc" + random_string`, but that's left as an exercise for the reader)

> ```go
> "--filter", "container=h
> ```

Because we expect one exec/exec_died event as there is an podman exec call above in the test. That is why we should test for exactly two matches as these are expected. It is just the healthcheck calls should not add more.

@cgiradkar Please make sure you add more context in the commit message, the one from th ePR is fine but it should match the commit message. And also add the `Fixes #17856` to the commit message and PR description so the issue correctly closed once thi gets merged.
